title: hedychium coccineum var. urophyllum
intro: Hedychium coccineum var. urophyllum is a variety of the Hedychium coccineum plant, commonly known as the orange ginger lily, a flowering plant native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including relief from coughs and colds, as well as reducing fever and inflammation. Hedychium coccineum var. urophyllum is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
